label: Initial migration — IDs updated to P#.YY.G# format
mission: Provide exceptional public safety services in a safe, compassionate, and professional manner.
vision: To be confirmed by command staff

pillars.name: Operational Readiness
pillars.purpose: Maintain and advance NPFR's capacity to respond safely and effectively to all emergency incidents within the community, with a well-equipped, well-positioned, and well-prepared operational force.

goals.statement: Evaluate the current deployment model against 36 months of incident data to identify gaps in response capability, unit utilization, and geographic coverage.
goals.metric: A completed deployment evaluation report reviewed and accepted by command staff, with gaps and recommendations documented.

tasks.text: Gather incident data from the last 36 months and separate by incident type.
Review and rank incident types by frequency, severity, and resource demand.
Evaluate the current deployment model against identified incident patterns.
Identify gaps in coverage, staffing configuration, or unit positioning.
Produce a deployment evaluation report with findings and recommendations.

goals.statement: Develop and implement a sustainable two-set PPE management program establishing reserve inventory standards, lifecycle replacement schedules, and long-term funding projections.
goals.metric: A documented PPE lifecycle replacement model with annual funding projections adopted and integrated into the budget process.

tasks.text: Conduct a comprehensive assessment of current PPE inventory including primary, secondary, reserve, and specialty sizing needs.
Develop minimum reserve PPE inventory standards to support recruit academies, new hires, damaged gear replacement, and emergency replacements.
Create a long-term PPE lifecycle replacement model accounting for the recent bulk purchase, projected retirements, and department growth.
Develop procedures within Honeywell First to monitor PPE assignment, serviceability, inspection compliance, and replacement forecasting.
Implement a sustainable PPE readiness program with annual review processes, reserve inventory targets, and replacement funding recommendations.

goals.statement: Develop and implement a major incident response planning program covering all identified threat types within NPFR's response model, aligned to deployment gaps identified in P1.26.G1.
goals.metric: Documented response plans for all identified major incident threat types, with at least one plan validated through a full-scale or tabletop exercise.

tasks.text: Identify major incident threat types from the deployment evaluation findings.
Review current response plans for alignment with identified threats and deployment model.
Develop a plan for each identified threat type including resource, staffing, and training requirements.
Plan and conduct a large-scale drill to test at least one major incident plan.
Establish a regular review and update cycle for all major incident plans.

goals.statement: Enhance hazardous materials response capability by expanding technician-level certification, developing a target hazard pre-plan library, and establishing a regular HOT training cadence.
goals.metric: Increased number of Hazmat Technician-certified personnel, a completed target hazard pre-plan library, and a documented annual training schedule.

tasks.text: Increase the number of personnel certified to the Hazardous Materials Technician level to serve as future FTOs.
Create a target hazard list for the city and develop incident response pre-plans for each site.
Establish annual or bi-annual HOT training and tabletop exercises led by Hazmat-certified FTOs.
Evaluate additional equipment needs to support safe response and mitigation within NPFR's scope.
Review and update any relevant policies or protocols to reflect capability changes.

goals.statement: Establish three strategically located logistics resource hubs using locker technology to improve access to EMS supplies, reserve equipment, and operational resources while reducing replenishment demands.
goals.metric: Three logistics resource hubs operational with documented SOGs, trained personnel, and a baseline effectiveness evaluation completed within the first year of operation.

tasks.text: Conduct a needs assessment and geographic analysis using call volume and resource utilization data from the deployment evaluation to identify three hub stations.
Develop a logistics resource hub program defining inventory categories, stocking levels, replenishment procedures, and accountability measures.
Purchase and install locker systems at designated hub locations and configure inventory management and access control processes.
Develop and implement SOGs and training for hub access, inventory accountability, and equipment tracking.
Evaluate program effectiveness by measuring replenishment frequency, labor hours, inventory availability, and user feedback.

goals.statement: Develop and implement a logistics operational resiliency program that eliminates single-point dependencies through knowledge management, cross-training, and continuity planning across all critical logistics functions.
goals.metric: Continuity guides completed for all identified single-point-of-dependency functions, with at least one annual exercise conducted and documented.

tasks.text: Identify critical logistics functions, systems, and responsibilities currently dependent on a single employee or position.
Develop position-specific continuity guides and SOPs for all critical logistics functions.
Establish a cross-training program providing secondary personnel with foundational knowledge of critical logistics responsibilities.
Develop a logistics division continuity plan identifying backup personnel, vendor contacts, and operating procedures for extended absences.
Conduct annual continuity exercises to validate documentation and assess cross-training effectiveness.

pillars.name: Workforce Development and Succession
pillars.purpose: Build a capable, credentialed, and career-ready workforce by investing in recruitment, training, professional development, and structured succession planning across all positions.

goals.statement: Identify all member development gaps, career pathways, and critical functions across the department to establish the foundation for succession and development planning.
goals.metric: A completed department-wide gap analysis report documenting all job functions, development needs, career pathways, and identified gaps, accepted by command staff.

tasks.text: Identify all job functions within the department.
Identify training and development needs associated with each function.
Identify all career pathways and critical succession roles.
Identify gaps between current capability and future need.
Produce a gap analysis report with findings and prioritized recommendations.

goals.statement: Develop and implement a formal recruitment and selection process aligned to operational needs, including targeted outreach, standardized interview criteria, a scoring system, and a recruitment committee.
goals.metric: A fully documented recruitment and selection process in place, with a recruitment committee established and at least one full hiring cycle completed under the new process.

tasks.text: Evaluate the prior fiscal year's applicant pool for quality, source, and diversity.
Partner with local technical schools and educational institutions to build a recruitment pipeline.
Develop an online recruitment portal and outreach materials including a QR code-based campaign.
Develop standardized interview questions, a documented scoring system, and establish a recruitment committee.
Evaluate the selection process against new employee performance at the 12-month review.

goals.statement: Develop and implement career development pathways and supporting policies for all department positions, based on the gap analysis findings from P2.26.G1.
goals.metric: Documented career development pathways for all positions published and distributed department-wide, with supporting policies adopted.

tasks.text: Develop pathways for member development covering all identified positions and career progressions.
Develop any needed policies or procedures governing pathway participation and progression.
Establish roles, responsibilities, and division oversight for the training function.
Present the development pathways to all department members.
Implement the pathways and establish a continuous evaluation process for workload, roles, and growth.

goals.statement: Develop and implement a department-wide succession plan covering all positions, built through multi-level organizational input and aligned to the career pathways established in P2.27.G1.
goals.metric: A completed, adopted succession plan document covering all identified critical positions, reviewed by command staff and integrated into the ODP.

tasks.text: Identify personnel projected to leave within the next five years.
Map specific career pathways for all succession-critical positions.
Develop the succession plan document using input from various levels of the organization.
Review the plan and incorporate feedback before finalizing.
Implement the succession plan and integrate it into the Officer Development Program.

goals.statement: Create a structured administrative development program that provides interested line personnel with meaningful exposure to departmental administrative functions, building internal bench strength before vacancies arise.
goals.metric: Program launched with documented structure, at least one cohort of participants completed, and a formal evaluation of outcomes conducted.

tasks.text: Identify administrative and specialty roles with historically difficult transitions or knowledge gaps.
Develop a structured rotation or shadow program giving line personnel access to administrative functions.
Build internal succession planning components into the program tied to the career pathways from P2.27.G1.
Create evaluation criteria to measure participant development and program effectiveness.
Encourage personnel to explore leadership paths before promotional timelines through active program promotion.

goals.statement: Achieve and maintain 90% or greater professional credentialing of all applicable positions aligned with CFAI credentialing categories, integrated into the succession plan and Officer Development Program.
goals.metric: 90% or greater of applicable positions credentialed within CFAI categories, with individual completion plans documented for all remaining positions.

tasks.text: Meet with all staff who have an associated CFAI credential category to assess current credentialing status.
Integrate credentialing requirements into the department succession plan document.
Develop an individual completion plan for all applicable staff members.
Incorporate credentialing milestones into the NPFR Officer Development Program.

goals.statement: Pursue regional recognition as a fire training resource by establishing institutional partnerships, gaining applicable accreditation, and hosting accredited training offerings for external agencies.
goals.metric: Regional accreditation obtained, at least two accredited training sessions hosted, and a 50% increase in collaborative training events with external agencies documented.

tasks.text: Establish and strengthen relationships with regional educational institutions and decision makers.
Establish or maintain articulation agreements with local institutions.
Reduce reliance on external training by formalizing shared resource agreements for training facilities.
Gain regional accreditation and host at least two accredited training sessions within the fiscal year.
Increase collaborative training events by 50% with external agencies through joint training agreements.

pillars.name: Personnel Health, Wellness, and Culture
pillars.purpose: Foster a department culture that actively supports the physical, behavioral, and professional well-being of every member, and maintains strong internal communication and cohesion at all levels.

goals.statement: Identify and assess department health and wellness needs through workforce survey, industry comparison, and facility evaluation, and report findings to inform program development.
goals.metric: A completed health and wellness needs assessment report with findings and program recommendations accepted by command staff.

tasks.text: Review all current relevant health and wellness policies.
Conduct a department-wide workforce survey on health, fitness, and wellness needs.
Compare survey results to industry standards and benchmarks.
Evaluate current fitness facilities at all stations.
Compile and report findings to command staff.

goals.statement: Develop and implement programs addressing behavioral health, PTSD support, cancer prevention, nutrition, fitness, and chaplain services, based on the findings from P3.26.G1.
goals.metric: At least three health and wellness programs formally adopted, funded, and operational with documented participation metrics.

tasks.text: Research programs in the following areas: firefighter cancer prevention, fitness, behavioral health, nutrition, and department chaplain.
Develop annual workforce task evaluations and an annual fitness assessment designed around position tasks.
Determine which programs align with the department's vision for health and wellness based on assessment findings.
Secure funding for adopted programs through budget requests and grant opportunities.
Implement adopted programs and establish review processes for ongoing effectiveness.

goals.statement: Enhance mental health and compassion communication training for all operational personnel, covering death notification, de-escalation, crisis intervention, and a formal qualifying incident support protocol.
goals.metric: 100% of operational personnel complete the training, and a qualifying incident support protocol is adopted and in place.

tasks.text: Identify current training gaps in death notification, de-escalation, and crisis communication.
Develop or procure specialized training curriculum covering all identified topic areas.
Deliver training to all operational personnel within the fiscal year.
Establish criteria for qualifying PTSD-related incidents and create a protocol for members requesting support time.
Implement the support protocol and follow up with data to assess effectiveness.

goals.statement: Develop and implement a department-wide internal communication program that improves the consistent flow of information between administrative and line personnel across all shifts and stations.
goals.metric: A formal internal communication program launched with at least two active channels, a documented station visit schedule, and a measured improvement in member awareness.

tasks.text: Identify current communication gaps and avenues for contact between officer and line personnel.
Develop a rotating schedule for administrative officers to visit each station throughout the year.
Explore creation of a regular department newsletter containing updates, training highlights, apparatus news, and member recognition.
Explore digital and social media platforms for short-form training and informational content.
Identify budget requirements for platforms and content development.

goals.statement: Establish and implement a station facilities service and replacement schedule for all high-use station equipment, covering both Facilities-managed and department-managed items.
goals.metric: A documented service and replacement schedule in place for all identified station equipment categories, with a corresponding budget line item established.

tasks.text: Review and categorize items serviced by Facilities and items serviced directly by the department.
Explore service contracts with vendors and review manufacturer-recommended service and replacement schedules.
Create a tracking and monitoring system to prompt timely service requests.
Create a budget item reflecting recurring service and replacement costs.

goals.statement: Evaluate the adequacy of current educational assistance funding relative to current tuition costs and internal career advancement requirements, and present findings and recommendations to city leadership.
goals.metric: A completed analysis presented to city leadership with a formal funding recommendation, and a documented outcome from that presentation.

tasks.text: Identify the current cost of college courses relative to prior years and collect educational requirements for internal upward movement.
Establish criteria an employee must meet to qualify for increased levels of educational assistance.
Compare current and historical data to validate the need for funding increases.
Present findings and a funding recommendation to city leadership.

pillars.name: Community Risk Reduction and Engagement
pillars.purpose: Proactively reduce community risk through data-driven prevention programs, public education, and meaningful engagement with residents, schools, and community partners across all age groups and risk profiles.

goals.statement: Gather and organize incident and community risk data across five task areas to establish a baseline for all CRR program development.
goals.metric: A completed, organized community risk dataset covering all five task areas, ranked by priority and available to the CRR team for analysis.

tasks.text: Pull and organize data from ImageTrend covering incident types and outcomes.
Integrate police reporting data for relevant risk categories.
Pull and organize GIS-based risk and demographic data.
Integrate Mobile Eyes and regional data sources.
Compile all data into a single organized dataset ranked by incident frequency and community impact.

goals.statement: Expand the car seat technician program by increasing certified personnel, improving scheduling and community availability, and building program succession to ensure long-term sustainability.
goals.metric: Increased number of certified technicians, documented improvement in scheduling availability, and a succession plan in place for the program.

tasks.text: Identify current certified technicians and assess gaps in coverage and availability.
Increase the number of certified Child Passenger Safety Technicians within the department.
Improve scheduling and availability for community events and inspection appointments.
Create a succession plan so the program remains sustainable as personnel turn over.

goals.statement: Evaluate and rank the community risk data gathered in P4.26.G1 to identify the highest-priority program areas and determine staffing, resource, and policy requirements for program development.
goals.metric: A prioritized CRR program development plan accepted by command staff, with identified staffing requirements and at least two new programs approved for development.

tasks.text: Organize and rank collected data by community impact, incident frequency, and prevention potential.
Evaluate the financial and organizational impact of addressing each identified risk area.
Establish programs based on greatest identified need.
Identify staffing requirements for program implementation including a second dedicated CRR staff position.
Formalize SOGs for the Fire Marshal function to support CRR program delivery.

goals.statement: Develop a fire explorer and cadet pathway program in partnership with local high schools that creates a structured pipeline for future firefighter recruitment.
goals.metric: A formal explorer or cadet program established with at least one partner high school, annual touchpoint events scheduled, and a baseline recruitment outcome metric defined.

tasks.text: Develop a Fire Explorer and cadet pathway with local high schools.
Create annual station visits and career days for students.
Identify whether the current cadet program produces measurable recruitment outcomes through school completion and job placement tracking.

goals.statement: Develop and implement a one-week youth summer camp in partnership with NPPD and Parks and Recreation that promotes safety awareness, community engagement, and positive interactions with NPFR personnel.
goals.metric: First summer camp session conducted with documented participation numbers, partner agreements in place, and a post-event evaluation completed.

tasks.text: Research similar youth camp models including the Charlotte County Sheriff's Office Junior Law Academy.
Identify target age group, staffing needs, facilities, transportation, and risk management requirements.
Meet with NPPD and Parks and Recreation to discuss partnership and shared staffing.
Develop program curriculum to include safety education, apparatus tours, team activities, and community service.

goals.statement: Establish a school zone fire department presence program at the start of each school year and return from long seasonal breaks to reinforce traffic safety awareness at high-risk crosswalks.
goals.metric: Program deployed at all identified high-risk school zone crosswalks for at least one start-of-year event, with NPPD coordination documented.

tasks.text: Review traffic patterns and identify high-risk crosswalks associated with each school zone in the city.
Review sunrise patterns and lighting conditions at affected crosswalks for early morning bus stops.
Include NPPD in the analysis and coordinate joint deployment where appropriate.
Develop signage for crews to display at intersections reinforcing school zone speed awareness.
Review and align shift schedules to coincide with deployment windows.

goals.statement: Develop and launch a comprehensive community risk reduction program with targeted interventions in fire prevention, drowning prevention, fall prevention, CPR training, and severe weather preparedness.
goals.metric: At least three formal CRR programs launched with documented reach metrics including number of participants, events, and geographic coverage.

tasks.text: Build out new program policies and content for the highest-priority risk areas identified in P4.27.G1.
Formalize elementary school CRR education programming.
Formalize senior public education programming.
Target outreach to align with incident data by ensuring program deployment matches geographic and demographic risk concentrations.
Begin phased program rollout across all identified priority areas.

goals.statement: Establish a community outreach team of 10 to 15 trained firefighters who can support public education presentations and CRR events across the community.
goals.metric: A functioning community outreach team of at least 10 trained members with documented event participation in the first year of operation.

tasks.text: Train firefighters interested in public education roles.
Create a roster of 10 to 15 members available to assist with presentations and events.
Analyze the internal and external impact of line personnel engaged in CRR activities.

goals.statement: Expand upon the three most successful CRR programs based on effectiveness data, improve weaker programs, and establish a state-level presence advocating for residential fire sprinkler requirements.
goals.metric: Documented expansion of three top-performing programs, improvement plans implemented for weaker programs, and at least one state-level engagement on residential sprinkler advocacy completed.

tasks.text: Access effectiveness data from ImageTrend, CARES database, GIS, regional sources, and external surveys.
Compare current program data to baseline and identify programs with the strongest outcomes.
Expand upon proven strengths in the top three performing programs.
Identify and implement improvement plans for the three weakest programs.
Strategize and establish a state-level presence to promote residential fire sprinkler requirements.

pillars.name: Clinical Excellence and EMS Innovation
pillars.purpose: Deliver the highest standard of emergency medical care through continuous quality improvement, advanced clinical training, and innovative service models that meet the evolving health needs of the North Port community.

goals.statement: Earn the AHA Mission Lifeline Gold Rating through consistent monitoring of cardiac care performance metrics, quarterly data reporting, and timely submission of all required measures.
goals.metric: AHA Mission Lifeline Gold Rating achieved and maintained through the fiscal year.

tasks.text: Monitor current and future cardiac care metrics on an ongoing basis.
Ensure protocols continue to meet best practices.
Conduct quarterly reporting of performance data.
Submit all required measures for Mission Lifeline Gold rating consideration.

goals.statement: Establish hospital clinical rotation opportunities for paramedics covering airway management, RSI exposure, surgical airway familiarity, and critical care observation.
goals.metric: At least one hospital partnership formalized with a documented rotation program and baseline participation numbers recorded in the first year.

tasks.text: Partner with local hospitals to establish optional or scheduled clinical rotations.
Define rotation scope covering airway management, intubation, RSI exposure, surgical airway familiarity, and critical care observation.
Develop scheduling and documentation protocols for rotation participation.

goals.statement: Develop and implement major incident response plans for EMS-specific threat types, including mass casualty events, ensuring protocols align with current best practices.
goals.metric: Documented EMS major incident response plans for all identified threat types, with at least one plan validated through an exercise.

tasks.text: Identify major incident threat types relevant to EMS operations.
Develop a plan for each identified EMS-specific threat type.
Plan and conduct a large-scale or tabletop exercise to validate at least one plan.
Re-evaluate plans following the exercise and identify additional needs.
Establish a regular review cycle to keep plans current.

goals.statement: Develop and implement a community paramedicine program that identifies high-utilization populations, establishes governing policies and practices, and launches a pilot program.
goals.metric: Community paramedicine program operational with documented policies, a defined patient population, and at least six months of utilization and outcome data collected.

tasks.text: Identify the benefits of a community paramedicine program and research models from comparable agencies.
Identify the areas and populations most in need and engage stakeholders interested in program development.
Establish policies and clinical practices governing program delivery.
Implement the program and begin tracking utilization and outcome data.

goals.statement: Analyze incident data from community paramedicine operations to identify populations where a mobile integrated health program would reduce resource utilization and improve outcomes, and establish the program framework.
goals.metric: MIH program framework adopted and operational with trained providers, at least one formal partner agreement in place, and baseline utilization data being tracked.

tasks.text: Analyze incident data to identify where MIH programs would have the greatest impact on resource allocation and non-transported patient outcomes.
Identify potential partnerships with local hospitals and third-party entities to become preferred providers for in-home care.
Establish a framework for MIH functions within the current response matrix.
Train and establish MIH-designated providers.

goals.statement: Evaluate the feasibility, return on investment, and strategic placement of a specialty care transport unit within the current response model, and establish response guidelines if the program moves forward.
goals.metric: A completed feasibility analysis and ROI assessment presented to command staff with a documented go or no-go recommendation.

tasks.text: Identify the ROI of specialty care transport within the current system using MIH utilization data.
Identify strategic placement of unit or units to best serve the community within the response model.
Establish response guidelines, a framework, and clinical protocols for a specialty care transport unit.

goals.statement: Strengthen relationships with local hospitals and healthcare systems to support joint education programs, MIH referral pathways, and collaborative community health initiatives.
goals.metric: At least two formal hospital partnerships documented with defined collaboration scope, and at least one joint community education event conducted.

tasks.text: Participate in meetings with hospital representatives to identify opportunities for joint education programs.
Identify potential for MIH referral partnerships with hospital and healthcare partners.
Strengthen existing relationships to improve communication and collaboration on community health initiatives.

pillars.name: Infrastructure, Technology, and Fiscal Stewardship
pillars.purpose: Maintain and improve the systems, facilities, technology, and fiscal practices that support NPFR's service delivery, ensuring reliability, accountability, and long-term organizational sustainability.

goals.statement: Modernize apparatus and fleet maintenance tracking by implementing a system that accurately captures complaint dates, repair timelines, return-to-service data, and key performance indicators for all vehicles.
goals.metric: A functional fleet tracking system capturing all defined KPIs, with a baseline year of data collected and an initial cost and downtime analysis completed.

tasks.text: Modernize the process for reporting mechanical issues to fleet to allow accurate tracking from complaint through return to service.
Develop a program to determine apparatus downtime and compare the PM schedule to manufacturer recommendations.
Calculate annual maintenance costs for fire suppression, EMS, and administrative vehicles and identify cost-saving opportunities.
Create KPIs including apparatus availability rate, reserve utilization, unscheduled repairs per call volume, average repair turnaround time, and parts procurement time.

goals.statement: Obtain and maintain ISO 1 rating and advance the department's CFAI accreditation through coordinated stakeholder engagement, infrastructure improvement, and documentation compliance.
goals.metric: ISO 1 rating maintained and CFAI accreditation status advanced with all required documentation submitted on schedule.

tasks.text: Coordinate with city directors and relevant partners on infrastructure requirements for ISO 1 maintenance.
Review criteria to maintain ISO 1 and assess all needs.
Advance CFAI accreditation — formalize accreditation SOG, implement processes, and gather proof of compliance.
Achieve CFAI applicant status.
Identify and pursue funding sources including grants and capital budget requests.

goals.statement: Analyze fleet maintenance cost and effectiveness data collected in P6.26.G1 to identify outsourcing opportunities, evaluate in-house EVT feasibility, and develop long-term cost reduction strategies.
goals.metric: A completed fleet cost and effectiveness analysis with specific recommendations on outsourcing and EVT feasibility presented to command staff and city leadership.

tasks.text: Analyze maintenance program effectiveness using tracking data from P6.26.G1.
Identify apparatus with the highest maintenance burden and cost.
Calculate and compare the cost of outsourcing preventive maintenance for passenger vehicles against in-house alternatives.
Evaluate the feasibility of hiring an in-house Emergency Vehicle Technician.
Present findings and recommendations to city leadership.

goals.statement: Evaluate and improve interoperability between scheduling and payroll platforms, specifically CrewSense and ExecuTime, to reduce administrative correction workload and improve payroll accuracy.
goals.metric: A documented reduction in payroll correction workload and measurable improvement in payroll accuracy following implementation.

tasks.text: Assess the current state of data exchange between CrewSense and ExecuTime and document known gaps and errors.
Engage city IT to identify integration solutions or middleware that can improve platform communication.
Implement improvements and measure impact on payroll accuracy and administrative correction workload.

goals.statement: Migrate departmental records to a centralized, access-controlled digital library that meets retention requirements, with proper file mapping, access controls, and disposition of records that have met retention.
goals.metric: All active departmental records migrated to the centralized library with a documented file map, access control structure, and a completed disposition log.

tasks.text: Meet with staff to develop a record inventory of all records created and maintained, and identify current storage locations.
Work with staff to develop a record library system including file mapping, access controls, and permission structures.
Migrate records that have not met retention from shared drives and local drives to the centralized library.
Inventory electronic records that have met retention, review with staff, and complete necessary disposals with the Clerk's office.

goals.statement: Create a Historical and Archive Committee to identify, catalogue, and maintain records and other items of historical significance to the department.
goals.metric: Committee established with a formal mandate and at least one complete inventory of historical items with documented storage and handling procedures.

tasks.text: Develop the committee mandate, scope, mission, goals, structure, and roles.
Identify potential members and recruit a committee.
Develop appropriate policies including collection, access and use, preservation and storage, and digitization and backup policies.
Inventory and evaluate existing items, determine appropriate storage and handling procedures, and identify space and resources needed.

goals.statement: Establish and maintain a formal periodic policy review process within PowerDMS ensuring 95% on-time completion, zero overdue reviews, and regulatory compliance across all departmental policies.
goals.metric: Zero overdue policy reviews in PowerDMS, 95% or higher on-time completion rate sustained, and all policies assigned to an owner and subject matter expert.

tasks.text: Assign each policy to the appropriate division and subject matter expert.
Achieve Policy Review Board evaluation and approval for all policies requiring updates within 60 days of submission.
Reduce the number of overdue policy reviews in PowerDMS to zero by year end, with receipting within 14 days.
Maintain a 95% or higher on-time policy review completion rate annually.

changelog.note: Initial migration to P#.YY.G# ID format. All goal IDs updated, predecessor references updated, statuses carried forward from vendor workbook baseline.
